title: Noggin
description: A noggin: a tree of work items with append-only notes, plus a cursor marking which item is currently active. YAML 1.2 is a JSON superset, so this schema validates both YAML and JSON renderings.

schemaVersion.description: Version of the noggin data model represented by this document.

active.description: Opaque key of the item currently marked active, or null when no item is active. When non-null, must reference an item in `items`; referential integrity is not expressible in pure JSON Schema and is the responsibility of the producer.

items.description: Flat list of items. Tree structure is implied via `parentKey`. Sibling order is array order.

ItemKey.description: Opaque, stable, never-reused item identifier. Treat as a string token; do not parse or assume a format.

Note.description: A single entry in an item's append-only note log.

timestamp.description: When the note was appended. May be null only for notes whose origin did not record a timestamp.

text.description: Note body. Free-form text. The reserved text `closed` records the transition of the parent item from open to done.

Item.description: A single work item in the noggin tree.

parentKey.description: Opaque key of the parent item, or null for root items. Multiple roots are allowed. When non-null, must reference an item in `items` and must not introduce a cycle; referential integrity and acyclicity are the responsibility of the producer.

done.description: False while the work is live, true once finished. The transition open→done is conventionally recorded by appending a note with text `closed`; the reverse transition does not modify notes.

createdAt.description: When the item was created. Optional in the data model; producers are encouraged to set it.

notes.description: Append-only log of `{ timestamp, text }` entries.
